sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date TIMESTAMPTZ, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, TIMESTAMPTZ '2023-05-01 12:00:00+00', 'Event A'), (2, TIMESTAMPTZ '2023-05-02 12:00:00+00', 'Event B'), (3, NULL, 'Event C');
SELECT id, date_part('year', event_date) + INTERVAL '1 DAY' AS adjusted_year FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, date_part('month', event_date) AS month, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY date_part('month', event_date)) AS month_count FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, date_part('day', event_date) + (CASE WHEN event_date IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS adjusted_day FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (date_part('year', event_date) * 365 + date_part('day', event_date)) + INTERVAL '5 DAY' AS total_days FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE events (event_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, related_date DATE, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO events VALUES (1, DATE '2023-05-01', 'Related Event A'), (2, DATE '2023-05-02', 'Related Event B');

SELECT d.id, d.description, (date_part('year', d.event_date) + date_part('day', e.related_date) + INTERVAL '5 DAY') AS mix_date FROM test_dates d JOIN events e ON date_part('year', e.related_date) = date_part('year', d.event_date) WHERE d.event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT event_id, COUNT(*) FROM events GROUP BY event_id HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL);
